methods.name: asCurves
methods.description: Returns the underlying curves that define the outline of the text. Calling this does not affect the SketchText and does not create any new sketch geometry but returns the geometrical definition of the sketch outline.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createForAssemblyContext
methods.description: Creates a proxy object for the SketchText object that represents the SketchText object in the context of an assembly. The context is defined by the input occurrence.

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Deletes the entity from the sketch.

methods.name: explode
methods.description: Explodes the SketchText into a set of curves. The original SketchText is deleted as a result of calling this.

methods.name: redefineAsAlongPath
methods.description: Sets this SketchTextInput to define text that follows along a specified path.

methods.name: redefineAsFitOnPath
methods.description: Sets this SketchTextInput to define text that fits along a specified path. Fitting on a path will space the characters so the text fits along the entire length of the path entity.

properties.name: angle
properties.description: **RETIRED** Gets and sets the angle of the text relative to the x-axis of the x-y plane of the sketch.

properties.name: assemblyContext
properties.description: Returns the assembly occurrence (i.e. the occurrence) of this object in an assembly. This is only valid in the case where this is acting as a proxy in an assembly. Returns null in the case where the object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

properties.name: attributes
properties.description: Returns the collection of attributes associated with this face.

properties.name: boundaryLines
properties.description: **RETIRED** Returns the four sketch lines that define the boundary of the sketch text. By adding constraints to these lines you can associatively control the size, position and angle of the sketch text.

properties.name: boundingBox
properties.description: Returns the bounding box of the entity in sketch space.

properties.name: definition
properties.description: Gets the definition that is currently used to specify how the sketch text is defined.

properties.name: entityToken
properties.description: Returns a token for the SketchEntity object. This can be saved and used at a later time with the Design.findEntityByToken method to get back the same sketch entity.   When using entity tokens it's important to understand that the token string returned for a specific entity can be different over time. However, even if you have two different token strings that were obtained from the same entity, when you use findEntityByToken they will both return the same entity. Because of that you should never compare entity tokens as way to determine what the token represents. Instead, you need to use the findEntityByToken method to get the two entities identified by the tokens and then compare them.

properties.name: fontName
properties.description: Gets and sets the name of the font to use.

properties.name: geometricConstraints
properties.description: Returns the sketch constraints that are attached to this curve.

properties.name: height
properties.description: **RETIRED** This property has been retired and you should instead use the heightParameter property to access the parameter controlling the text height and use its properties to get and set the height.

properties.name: heightParameter
properties.description: Returns the model parameter that was created when the sketch text was created that controls the height of the sketch text. To edit the height, you can use the expression and value properties of the returned ModelParameter object.

properties.name: is2D
properties.description: Indicates if this curve lies entirely on the sketch x-y plane.

properties.name: isDeletable
properties.description: Indicates if this sketch entity can be deleted. There are cases, especially with sketch points where another entity is dependent on an entity so deleting it is not allowed. For example, you can't delete the center point of circle by itself but deleting the circle will delete the point. The same is true for the end points of a line.

properties.name: isFixed
properties.description: Indicates if this geometry is "fixed".

properties.name: isFullyConstrained
properties.description: Indicates if this sketch entity is fully constrained.

properties.name: isHorizontalFlip
properties.description: Gets and sets if the text is flipped horizontally.

properties.name: isLinked
properties.description: Indicates if this sketch entity was created by a projection, inclusion, or driven by an API script. If this returns true, then the entity is presented to the user as not editable and with a 'break link' command available.

properties.name: isReference
properties.description: Indicates if this geometry is a reference. Changing this property from true to false removes the reference. This property can not be set to true if it is already false.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: isVerticalFlip
properties.description: Gets and sets if the text is flipped vertically.

properties.name: isVisible
properties.description: When a sketch is created, geometry is sometimes automatically added to the sketch. For example a sketch point that references the origin point is always included and if a face was selected to create the sketch on, geometry from the face is also included. This automatically created geometry behaves in a special way in that it is invisible but is available for selection and it also participates in profile calculations. It's not possible to make them visible but they can be deleted and they can be used for any other standard sketch operation.

properties.name: nativeObject
properties.description: The NativeObject is the object outside the context of an assembly and in the context of it's parent component. Returns null in the case where this object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parentSketch
properties.description: Returns the parent sketch.

properties.name: position
properties.description: **RETIRED** Gets and sets the position of the text on the x-y plane of the sketch. The text must lie on the x-y plane so the Z component of the point is ignored and always treated as zero.

properties.name: referencedEntity
properties.description: Returns the referenced entity in the case where IsReference is true. However, this property can also return null when IsReference is true in the case where the reference is not parametric.

properties.name: sketchDimensions
properties.description: Returns the sketch dimensions that are attached to this curve.

properties.name: text
properties.description: **RETIRED** This property has been retired and you should now use the textValue and expression properties of the Parameter object associated with this SketchText, which you can obtain by using the SketchText.textParameter property.

properties.name: textParameter
properties.description: Returns the model parameter that was created when the sketch text was created that controls the contents of the sketch text. To edit the text, you can use the expression and textValue properties of the returned ModelParameter object.

properties.name: textStyle
properties.description: Gets and sets the text style to apply to the entire text. This is a bitwise enum so styles can be combined to apply multiple styles. For example you can apply bold and underline.

related_samples.name: Sketch Text API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating sketch text by creating both mult-line text and text along a curve.

# Get the value of the property.

asCurves.title: SketchText.asCurves Method
asCurves.description: Returns the underlying curves that define the outline of the text. Calling this does not affect the SketchText and does not create any new sketch geometry but returns the geometrical definition of the sketch outline.

syntax.python: returnValue = sketchText_var.asCurves()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = sketchText_var->asCurves();

asCurves.return_description: Returns an array of transient curves that represent the outline of the text.

classType.title: SketchText.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.fusion.SketchText.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::fusion::SketchText::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createForAssemblyContext.title: SketchText.createForAssemblyContext Method
createForAssemblyContext.description: Creates a proxy object for the SketchText object that represents the SketchText object in the context of an assembly. The context is defined by the input occurrence.

syntax.python: returnValue = sketchText_var.createForAssemblyContext(occurrence)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = sketchText_var->createForAssemblyContext(occurrence);

createForAssemblyContext.return_description: Returns the proxy object or null if this isn't the NativeObject.

parameters.name: occurrence
parameters.description: The occurrence that defines the context to create the proxy in.

deleteMe.title: SketchText.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: Deletes the entity from the sketch.

syntax.python: returnValue = sketchText_var.deleteMe()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = sketchText_var->deleteMe();

deleteMe.return_description: Returns true is the delete was successful.

explode.title: SketchText.explode Method
explode.description: Explodes the SketchText into a set of curves. The original SketchText is deleted as a result of calling this.

syntax.python: returnValue = sketchText_var.explode()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = sketchText_var->explode();

explode.return_description: Returns an array of the sketch curves that were created that represent the text.

redefineAsAlongPath.title: SketchText.redefineAsAlongPath Method
redefineAsAlongPath.description: Sets this SketchTextInput to define text that follows along a specified path.

syntax.python: returnValue = sketchText_var.redefineAsAlongPath(path, isAbovePath, horizontalAlignment, characterSpacing)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = sketchText_var->redefineAsAlongPath(path, isAbovePath, horizontalAlignment, characterSpacing);

redefineAsAlongPath.return_description: Returns true if the setting the definition was successful.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The entity that defines the path for the text. This can be a SketchCurve or BRepEdge object.

parameters.name: isAbovePath
parameters.description: Indicates if the text should be positioned above or below the path entity.

parameters.name: horizontalAlignment
parameters.description: Specifies the horizontal alignment of the text with respect to the path curve.

parameters.name: characterSpacing
parameters.description: The spacing between the characters. This is an additional spacing to apply that is defined as a percentage of the default spacing. A spacing of 0 indicates no additional spacing. A spacing of 50 indicates to use the default plus 50% of the default.

redefineAsFitOnPath.title: SketchText.redefineAsFitOnPath Method
redefineAsFitOnPath.description: Sets this SketchTextInput to define text that fits along a specified path. Fitting on a path will space the characters so the text fits along the entire length of the path entity.

syntax.python: returnValue = sketchText_var.redefineAsFitOnPath(path, isAbovePath)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = sketchText_var->redefineAsFitOnPath(path, isAbovePath);

redefineAsFitOnPath.return_description: Returns true if the setting the definition was successful.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The entity that defines the path for the text. This can be a SketchCurve or BRepEdge object.

parameters.name: isAbovePath
parameters.description: Indicates if the text should be positioned above or below the path entity.
